exampleContent.sectionSubtitle: Real prompts and articles from this template

prompts.title: Example Action Prompts
prompts.subtitle: Practical tasks to move your photography business forward
items: What specific photography style and niche truly excites you, and how does this align with the market demand in your area? Consider not just what you enjoy shooting, but what clients are actively seeking and willing to pay for.

What essential photography equipment do you currently own, what gaps exist in your gear for your chosen niche, and how will you prioritize equipment investments within your startup budget? Consider both the technical requirements of your target clients and the return on investment each piece of equipment will provide.

What are your current technical skills and creative abilities as a photographer, and where do you need to develop further expertise before launching your business? Consider both the technical aspects (lighting, composition, post-processing) and business skills (client communication, workflow management, brand development) required for your chosen photography niche.

What legal and business structure foundation do you need to establish for your photography business, and how will you protect both your creative work and business operations? Consider business registration, insurance coverage, contracts and liability protection, copyright ownership, and tax implications for your chosen business structure.

What is your unique value proposition as a photographer, and how will you differentiate yourself from competitors in your local market? Consider your artistic style, client experience approach, specialized techniques, target audience understanding, and the specific problems you solve that other photographers in your area might not address.

What are the fundamental business operations and workflows you need to establish to deliver consistent, professional photography services? Consider your client consultation process, booking and scheduling systems, shoot preparation protocols, delivery timelines, payment processing, file organization and backup strategies, and quality control standards.
